facts.report_excerpt: = Analysing Report :description: Current owner-oriented maturity report for the Analysing component. :page-slug: analysing-report This page is the current owner-oriented maturity report for *Analysing*. It is written not as a defect log, but as a public-facing readiness portrait: what the component already demonstrates, what maturity band it currently occupies, and which leader-class direction it is converging toward. == Component Portrait Analysing is not shaped as a simple reporting script bundle or a lightweight analysis helper. It reads as a Symfony-oriented analytics and analysis-process component: a system intended to manage analytics-oriented application runtime, Doctrine-backed processing, scan-layer mapping, namespace and domain-hygiene control, and guarded structural evolution around analytical logic. The currently visible public slice is thinner than in some of the more mature ecosystem repositories, but even that thinner slice already shows signs of being treated as a governed subsystem rather than as a convenience utility. Its current shape suggests an internal analytics and insight-runtime platform layer designed for structured evolution and integration into a wider ecosystem. == Ecosystem Role Within the broader Smartresponsor direction, Analysing appears to occupy the role of an analytics, analysis-process, and structural-guard component. Its ecosystem role can be understood across several functions: * as an analytics-oriented application runtime, * as a scan and layer-map surface, * as a namespace and domain-hygiene enforcement layer, * as a Doctrine-backed processing component, * and as a reusable internal analytical capability for broader applications. This makes Analysing strategically more important than a typical dashboard helper or local report generator. It points toward a reusable internal analytics capability that other components can depend on. == Current Maturity Analysing already presents several useful signals of maturity: * a clear analytics-process product identity, * explicit PHP 8.4, Symfony, Doctrine ORM, and DBAL targeting, * PHPUnit, PHPStan, and php-cs-fixer quality posture, * dedicated scan, canon, namespace, domain-hygiene, and PHP-header guard scripts, * and an application stack that already reads as runtime-capable rather than as a one-off analysis utility. At the same time, the currently visible public slice is thinner than for components like Billing, Shipping, Paying, or Ordering. The README is not present, and the publicly exposed business-surface evidence is comparatively light. So the current reading is strong in structural discipline and architectural intent, but thinner in public runtime proof and domain-surface richness. == RC Scorecard [cols="2,1,4",options="header"] |=== | Layer | Score to RC | Reading | Product identity | 7.9 / 10 | The component is already clearly framed as an analytics and analysis-process application rather than a generic helper layer. | Architecture and domain shape | 8.1 / 10 | Symfony, Doctrine, and guard-driven structural controls already form a coherent analysis-runtime architecture. | Runtime and application surface | 7.8 / 10 | The repo clearly targets application runtime, but the currently visible public runtime evidence is still thinner than in the strongest ecosystem components. | QA maturity | 7.9 / 10 | PHPUnit, PHPStan, and cs posture are present, though the visible QA contour remains lighter than the most mature repositories. | Governance and operational readiness | 8.4 / 10 | Scan-layer mapping, namespace guard, domain-hygiene guard, and header/canon controls form one of the component’s clearest strengths. | Analytics and insight posture | 8.0 / 10 | The component already reads as insight-oriented runtime rather than passive reporting, but public domain-surface richness is still developing. | Market-facing alignment | 7.8 / 10 | The component already aligns with enterprise analytics and insight-runtime classes, though it still reads more as a structured platform seed than a finished public analytics suite. | Overall readiness to RC | 8.0 / 10 | Analysing is a promising pre-RC component with strong structural discipline and thinner current public evidence. |=== == Leader Line Analysing should not be measured against simple reporting scripts or spreadsheet-style exports. Its natural comparison line is closer to the enterprise-class analytics, product insight, and business-intelligence runtime category, including the kind of capability bands represented by: * centralized insight generation, * structural and event-aware analysis runtime, * product and operational analytics posture, * governed analytical processing, * and disciplined handling of analytical application structure. This means the component is better understood as targeting the same strategic class of problem addressed by stronger analytics and BI leaders, while expressing that class through a Symfony-native internal component. In that sense, the leader line …

verdict.summary: Analysing reads as a Symfony/Doctrine-backed analytics runtime with strong internal guards and containerized ops, but it lacks a README, visible CI evidence, and release tagging to confidently claim RC.
strengths: Symfony + Doctrine application skeleton with public entrypoint and console
Containerization and Nginx configs for a runnable local environment
Robust internal guards (namespace, domain-hygiene, header) and QA tooling (PHPStan, PHPUnit, CS Fixer, Psalm)
Smoke and unit tests exist alongside a QA environment check script
risks: No README or public quickstart; external/product story is thin
CI/CD status unclear despite workflows directory
No git tags/releases; maturity and change history are opaque
Playwright config exists without package.json, suggesting e2e harness is not wired in this repo
next_actions: Add a README with quickstart, run commands, architecture outline, and domain map links
Define and enable GitHub Actions to run qa:env, phpunit, phpstan, cs-fixer (dry-run), guards, and gitleaks on PRs
Introduce semantic version tags and a CHANGELOG, and create a release workflow
Either wire Playwright e2e (add package.json and CI job) or remove the unused config
Document container runbook (docker-compose up, health check, URLs) and add a CI smoke boot test
Promote docs/ content into the public docs site (or add a lightweight Antora module) and link from README
notes: Assessment grounded only in repository files and current snapshot; no external tool outputs parsed
Component is enabled in registry; scheduled assessment runner integration is assumed but not evidenced here
Docs exist under docs/, but no README and no Antora nav/index are present in this repo
phpunit.xml.dist is present; prefer a concrete phpunit.xml (or CI override) with coverage thresholds

product.summary: Product identity as an analytics/analysis-process Symfony app is clear from composer metadata, layered src layout, and focused docs, though public-facing narrative remains thin.
risks: No README or quickstart blurs external understanding of value and scope
Absence of release tags weakens perceived product maturity
improvements: Add a README with positioning, quickstart, example flows, and links to docs/
Publish a minimal feature map and usage examples to clarify boundaries

architecture.summary: A coherent Symfony + Doctrine architecture is present with layered directories and custom guard scripts enforcing namespaces, domain hygiene, and headers.
risks: Architectural intent is implied by structure/guards but not formalized in ADRs
Guards rely on manual/script execution unless wired into CI
improvements: Add a short architecture/ADR note in docs/ to capture decisions and boundaries
Run all guard scripts in CI to keep structure enforced continuously

runtime.summary: Deployable runtime surface is evident (public/index.php, Kernel, routes, bin/console) with Docker and Nginx for local execution and smoke tests in tests/.
risks: No README/runbook leaves runtime steps implicit
Pipeline boot/health checks are not evidenced
Playwright config without package.json suggests e2e browser tests are not wired
improvements: Document run commands and endpoints in README and add a CI smoke boot test against the container
Either wire Playwright (add package.json and CI job) or remove the unused config
Expose and document a simple health endpoint checked in CI

qa.summary: Testing and static analysis are configured (PHPUnit, PHPStan, Psalm, CS Fixer) with smoke and unit tests plus a QA environment checker.
risks: Only phpunit.xml.dist is present; CI configuration and coverage thresholds are not visible
No published CI results or coverage report in the repo
improvements: Add GitHub Actions to run qa:env, phpunit with coverage, and phpstan at level max
Commit or CI-generate a phpunit.xml with coverage thresholds and report artifacts
Include Psalm in CI to complement PHPStan

ops.summary: Operational and governance posture is strong: container assets, secrets scanning, code hygiene/guard scripts, and a workflows directory for CI are present.
risks: Actual CI job definitions and deployment automation are not evidenced
No release tags/changelog reduce operational traceability of changes
improvements: Add CI pipelines for static analysis, tests, guards, and gitleaks on PRs
Add image build/push steps and a lightweight release workflow with semantic tags and CHANGELOG

market.summary: The component aligns with an internal analytics/insight platform role and includes some docs, but outward-facing product narrative and packaging are minimal.
risks: Proprietary license limits external adoption possibilities
Missing README and no releases/tags obscure market readiness
improvements: Add a README with value proposition and integration examples
Introduce versioned releases and minimal change notes to signal stability

overall.summary: Analysing shows strong structural and ops hygiene with a real runtime scaffold; wiring CI, release tagging, and a README would close the remaining pre-RC gaps.
risks: Lack of README and public runbook
CI execution and coverage thresholds not evidenced
No version tags/releases
improvements: Stand up CI for tests/analysis/guards and publish artifacts
Add semantic version tags and a CHANGELOG
Provide a concise README and link existing docs for onboarding
